query: Can you review my PR before I merge it? I added a new AddItem command to the Cart service and a CartItemAdded event — I want to make sure there are no ES/CQRS violations, that the decide function ends correctly, and that I haven't accidentally edited any locked files.

look over this branch im merging into main — feature/stock-reservation — I added StockReserved to the event ADT but im not sure if i updated the update fold in Entity.hs to cover it, and i also touched the StockLevel query so want to check the combine is still total
pls check my diff, i added a new quantity field to CartEntity and want to make sure its safe for event replay — worried it might break old snapshots if its not Maybe or doesnt have a json default

Diff review for feature/library-checkout: the PR touches src/Library/Commands/CheckoutBook.hs, src/Library/Events/BookCheckedOut.hs, and src/Library/Queries/LoanStatus.hs — can you go through each file and give me severity-ranked findings before I ask for approval?

I need you to look over my changes on the counter-improvements branch — specifically I'm worried the new CounterReset event name might be too vague, and I also wrote some of the decide logic ending in pure [] by accident instead of a Decider constructor. Catch any other blockers too.

check my code before merging: I updated the StockLevel query and I'm pretty sure I also touched Events/StockReserved.hs which might already be in .locked-files — can you run through the diff and tell me if there are any blockers or immutability violations I need to fix first?

Audit my Cart integration branch for vanilla Haskell leakage — I wrote parts of the CartIntegration module using String and IO instead of Text and Task because I was copying from an old snippet, and I want to know every line in the diff that needs fixing before the reviewer sees it.

My tech lead wants a formal review of PR #87 on the Library service. It touches Service.hs, App.hs, adds BookReserved and BookCancelled events, and introduces a new ReservationSummary query. Please scope to the diff, classify each changed file, and produce file:line findings ranked blocker through nit.

I have Commands/IncrementCounter.hs in .locked-files and I need to add a step field to it. Walk me through the full V2 versioning process — what to name the new file, what type name to use inside it, and how to add the variant to the event ADT without touching the original.

Can you help me set up a GitHub Actions workflow so that every pull request on the Library repo automatically runs the NeoHaskell code review checks and posts inline comments? I want the CI pipeline to enforce the ES/CQRS rules on every merge attempt.

I need to verify my event-model.json for the Stock module is internally consistent — check that every edge sourceId and targetId references a real node, that event names are past-tense, that commands are imperative, and that no infrastructure is modeled as a node.

Help me write the Decider spec unit tests for the new CheckoutBook command in the Library service — I need a test for the happy path where the book is available and one for the rejection case where it's already on loan.

I added a new discountApplied boolean field to CartEntity and the app is crashing on startup replaying old events. Walk me through using expand-entity correctly so the field gets a safe default and old snapshots don't break during replay.

My LoanStatus query is missing the canAccess and canView declarations and it won't compile. Show me how to implement them — specifically whether to use publicAccess vs authenticatedAccess vs ownerOnly for a query that should only be visible to the borrower.

I'm about to model the Library checkout flow from scratch — help me design the commands, events, and queries in the event model before I write any code. I want to get the boundaries right between CheckoutBook, BookCheckedOut, and the LoanStatus read model.

Walk me through wiring the new ReserveStock command into Service.hs and adding the Application.withQuery call for StockLevel — I want to make sure the service registration is correct and the query endpoint is reachable.
